ref: Before there were kings in Israel, the following kings ruled Edom one after another: Bela son of Beor from Dinhabah; Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah; Husham from the land of Teman; Hadad son of Bedad from Avith ; Samlah from Masrekah; Shaul from the city of Rehoboth on the Euphrates River; Baalhanan son of Achbor; Hadar from the city of Pau .
text: Menli ɛhye mɔ lile arelemgbunli wɔ Yidɔm kolaa na Yizilayɛma anyia arelemgbunli azie bɛ. Biyɔ ara Bila mɔɔ vi Denehabaa Zilaa ara Dwobabe mɔɔ vi Bɔzelaa Huhyam mɔɔ vi Timan la Bidade ara Heedade mɔɔ hunle Medeanema wɔ Mowabe kpɔkɛ nu la. Ɔvi Evete. Samelaa mɔɔ vi Maselika Hyɔɔlo mɔɔ vi Rilihɔbo mɔɔ wɔ Azule Yufileti ne anloa la. Akebɔ ara Beeyale Heenan Heedade mɔɔ vi Peewu, Mɛhɛtabɛle ɔ nli a le Meetelɛde yɛɛ ɔ nla a le Meezahabe.
translated: Menli is the name of the place where the people of Yidɔm live and Yizilayɛma people also live there.  Biyɔ ara Bila is the name of the place where Denehabaa Zilaa lives. Dwobabe is the name of the place where Bɔzelaa Huhyam lives. Timan is the name of the place where Bidade lives. Heedade is the name of the place where Medeanema lives in Mowabe.  Evete. Samelaa is the name of the place where Maselika Hyɔɔlo lives. Rilihɔbo lives in Azule Yufileti and anloa. Akebɔ ara Beeyale Heenan Heedade is the name of the place where Peewu lives, Mɛhɛtabɛle is the name of the place where Meetelɛde lives. Meezahabe is the name of the place where the people live.

text: Na Ɛdɛnkɛma Nyamenle manle mbaka kɔsɔɔti mɔɔ le ngɛnlɛma nee mɔɔ bɛdi a ɔyɛ boɛ la fifile; ɔmanle ngoane baka ne yɛɛ baka ne mɔɔ maa ɛkola ɛnwu kpalɛ nee ɛtane la ɔdaye bɛfifile tola ne anu.
translated: When the people of Edɛnkɛma Nyamenle were in a state of great hunger, they went to the forest to look for food. They found a large, ripe fruit and ate it.  They ate so much that they became very sick and their stomachs ached.

translated: This is the story of the Belemgbunli Kɛdɔleewomɛ people. They are known for their bravery and strength. They are strong and courageous, and they will fight for what is right.

text: Gyekɔbo bizale bɛ kɛ, “Me mediema, nienwu a bɛvi a?” Bɛbuale ye kɛ, “Yɛvi Hɛlan.”
translated: Gyekɔbo asked, "My mother, who is the one who is going to die?" They replied, "It is Hɛlan."

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: “Menli ɛhye mɔ le agɔnwolɛma. Bɛmaa bɛ adenle bɛmaa bɛdɛnla azɛlɛ ye azo na bɛli bɛ gua, ɔluakɛ azɛlɛ ye le kpole maa bɛ. Bɛmaa yɛgya bɛ mra ne mɔ bie na bɛdabɛ noko bɛgya yɛ mra mraalɛ ne mɔ bie.
translated: The man is a hunter. They will give him a gun, they will give him a knife to hunt with so that he can get food. They will give him a net to catch fish and he will give them some of the fish. They will give him a trap to catch animals and he will give them some of the animals.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Dwazo, kɔmaa kakula ne zo na nea ye boɛ kpalɛ, ɔluakɛ mebamaa yeayɛ maanle kpole.”

text: Liya bizale ye kɛ, “Ɛdwenle kɛ ɔle nzɛmedɛ kɛ wɔza me wɔlie me hu la ɔ? Yeanga ye zɔhane, eza ɛbalie me ra ye mandileki ne noko.” Relahyɛle buale ye kɛ, “Ɛ ra ne mandileki ne ati, ɛnɛ nɔe Gyekɔbo nee wɔ bala.”
translated: Liya said, “Is it true that you want to kill me? I will not allow you to kill me. I will fight you and my people.” Then Gyekɔbo replied, “It is true that I want to kill you and your people, but I will not kill you.”

ref: We have to do only one thing before they will agree to stay here and become one nation with us. Our men will have to be circumcised just like theirs.
text: Mɔɔ menli ɛhye mɔ kpondɛ na bɛadɛnla yɛ avinli bɛ nee yɛ ayɛ ko la a le kɛ, yɛ mrenya ne mɔ, ko biala kɛyɛ ye mrenyanu nzonlɛ, kɛmɔ bɛyɛ bɛ ɛdeɛ ne la.
translated: If you do not listen to the advice, you will be in trouble. You will regret it later, because the advice is good. It is better to listen to the advice than to regret it later.

text: Ayezeke lile kenle mɔtwɛ la, Ebileham yɛle ye mrenyanu nzonlɛ, kɛmɔ Nyamenle zele ye la.
translated: The one who is like a king among men, Ebileham, he is the one who is like Nyamenle.
